intended_use: The Senhance Surgical System is intended to assist in the accurate control of laparoscopic instruments for visualization and endoscopic manipulation of tissue including grasping, cutting, blunt and sh
intended_use_full: The Senhance Surgical System is intended to assist in the accurate control of laparoscopic instruments for visualization and endoscopic manipulation of tissue including grasping, cutting, blunt and sharp dissection, approximation, ligation, electrocautery, suturing, mobilization and retraction. The Senhance Surgical System is intended for use in laparoscopic gynecological surgery, colorectal surgery, cholecystectomy, and inguinal hernia repair. The system is indicated for adult use. It is intended for use by trained physicians in an operating room environment in accordance with the Instructions for Use.
target_condition: Conditions requiring laparoscopic gynecological surgery, colorectal surgery, cholecystectomy, and inguinal hernia repair
ground_truth: Surgical outcomes assessed through chart review including operative time, complications, conversions, readmissions, and reoperations. Reference comparisons made against published literature data from da Vinci robotic system and standard laparoscopic technique.
other_metrics: Cholecystectomy (Senhance): complication rate (non-serious) 0-30%, serious complications 0%, intraoperative complications 0-5%, conversion to laparoscopy 5%, conversion to open 0%, mortality 0%, operative time 71.5-86.5 min, hospital length of stay 0-2 days. Inguinal Hernia Repair (Senhance, N=64): intraoperative complications 0%, postoperative complications 1.6%, readmission rate 0%, reoperation rate 0%, mortality 0, operative time 44±17.4 min (unilateral) and 79±23.7 min (bilateral).
